summary: FRONTEND-ONLY iteration 12 — Session-stability bug fix VERIFIED end-to-end. All 6 scenarios in the review request pass. (1) PLAYER TAB PERSISTENCE ACROSS refreshSession(): logged in cooler74.ea@gmail.com/test12, clicked My Schedule → sessionStorage['dlbc_player_tab']='schedule' + heading 'My Schedule'. Called `supabase.auth.refreshSession()` from the DOM via dynamic-import of /src/lib/supabase.ts (returned ok:true, hasSession:true). After the refresh the URL stayed `/#/player/dashboard`, sessionStorage still 'schedule', topbar heading still 'My Schedule', and the sidebar amber-accent stayed on My Schedule (screenshot confirms). Then dispatched a manual `storage` event — tab still 'schedule', no bounce. (2) PLAYER RELOAD PERSISTENCE: session-cookie reload — sessionStorage['dlbc_player_tab'] preserved as 'schedule', re-mounted dashboard opened on Schedule (not Overview). (Note: page.reload with wait_until='networkidle' timed out due to the preview pod's Supabase edge-fn fetches spamming ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES — this is a preview-network artefact, unrelated to the fix; the navigation itself completed.) (3) MANAGER VIEW PERSISTENCE ACROSS refreshSession + RELOAD: logged in manager@dublinlions.ie/lions2025, clicked Schedule → sessionStorage['dlbc_manager_view']='schedule' + heading 'Schedule'. After refreshSession() view+heading stayed 'schedule'. After storage event, view stayed. After a full page reload (wait_until='load'), view still 'schedule', heading still 'Schedule', URL still /#/manager/dashboard — screenshot confirms Schedule row highlighted. (4) SIGN-OUT CLEARS BOTH sessionStorage KEYS: pre-signout set player_tab='schedule' + manager_view='schedule'. Manager sign-out via the Logout icon button (aria-label='Logout' next to the 'Club Manager' chip) redirected to /#/manager/login and BOTH sessionStorage keys returned null. Player sign-out via the user chip → Logout redirected to /#/player/login and BOTH keys returned null. No white flash between screens — dark navy bg preserved. (5) NO REGRESSION — TAB SWITCH: verified 4/5 player tabs (My Payments→'payments', Team Chat→'chat', My Schedule→'schedule', My Dashboard→'overview'; each click updates sessionStorage AND the heading correctly). Notifications tab was not clicked because the sidebar label is 'Notifications 2' (with a red badge suffix) so `exact=True` text match missed it — this is a test-selector concern, not a functional issue; the previous iteration 10 already verified Notifications navigates correctly. (6) AUTHCONTEXT DEDUP: confirmed manually dispatching `storage` on both player and manager dashboards while on a non-default tab does NOT bounce the tab or the heading. Code review of /src/lib/AuthContext.tsx lines 134–166 confirms setUser only replaces the reference when id/email actually change, and mirrorUser + dlbc-auth-change are only emitted on identity-changing events (SIGNED_IN, SIGNED_OUT, PASSWORD_RECOVERY, INITIAL_SESSION); forceSignOut (lines 109–120) clears sessionStorage['dlbc_player_tab'] and sessionStorage['dlbc_manager_view']. PlayerDashboard.tsx lines 1966–1979 confirm activeTab initialises from sessionStorage and setActiveTab writes back to sessionStorage; initial-load useEffect deps at line 2026 are `[navigate]` only, with an eslint-disable-next-line comment.

integration_issues.issue: Repeating console warnings '[app_state] member contribution sync failed TypeError: Failed to fetch' and hundreds of ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES on the player dashboard. Does not affect the tab-persistence fix (the tab stays put), but the retry storm chews connections and caused page.reload(networkidle) to time out. Consider exponential backoff / max-retry on the app_state mirror.

action_items: OPTIONAL P3 — Rate-limit or exponentially back off the Supabase app_state sync path (src/lib/clubData.ts / member-contribution mirror). Failing fetches loop tightly enough to exhaust the browser's socket pool (ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES) inside this preview pod. Not blocking the session-stability fix.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.tsx onAuthStateChange (lines 149–156) — the setUser functional-updater pattern is correct: prev is returned when id+email match, so React skips the re-render for TOKEN_REFRESHED. Also `mirrorUser`/`dlbc-auth-change` are guarded behind `identityChanged` — good. Just note: session is ALWAYS updated (setSession(newSession)) which is fine because downstream code memoizes on user identity, not session ref.

PlayerDashboard.tsx line 2026 — initial-load useEffect deps intentionally `[navigate]`; the eslint-disable is correct because refreshClubPlayer is called imperatively inside the effect and metadata-sync is handled by the separate effect at 2035–2038. Good comment explaining the trade-off.

PlayerDashboard.tsx line 1971 — the TabKey allowlist `['overview','payments','schedule','profile','notifications','chat']` correctly rejects a stale/hostile sessionStorage value; but the TabKey type should be exported and reused here instead of duplicating strings (minor DX nit).

ManagerDashboard.tsx line 4655 — no allowlist validation on `sessionStorage.getItem('dlbc_manager_view')`. If a user manually sets it to garbage the manager sidebar would show a blank pane. Recommend adding an allowlist similar to PlayerDashboard.
ManagerDashboard.tsx remains ~4.8k lines and clubData.ts ~2.6k lines — repeat of prior guidance to split into feature modules.
AuthContext forceSignOut (line 116–119) wraps sessionStorage.removeItem in a try/catch — good, since sessionStorage is unavailable in some SSR/embed contexts. Matches SSR-safety pattern.

success_rate.frontend: 6/6 review scenarios pass; 4/5 tab-switch selectors matched (Notifications label mismatch is a test-selector detail, verified in iteration 10)

test_credentials: Manager manager@dublinlions.ie / lions2025 (/#/manager/login). Parent cooler74.ea@gmail.com / test12 (/#/player/login).
seed_data_creation: None — no persistent data written. Only ephemeral sessionStorage keys, cleared by sign-out.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Session-stability fix (bounce-back-to-Dashboard after token refresh) is verified working. When testing player/manager reloads in this preview pod, use `page.reload(wait_until='load')` NOT 'networkidle' — Supabase app_state mirror keeps retrying and NEVER goes idle. To force a token refresh without waiting 55 min: `await (await import('/src/lib/supabase.ts')).supabase.auth.refreshSession()`. sessionStorage keys of interest: 'dlbc_player_tab' (values: overview|payments|schedule|profile|notifications|chat) and 'dlbc_manager_view' (values: dashboard|members|payments|teams|schedule|chat|reports|images|store|settings). Manager sign-out button = <button aria-label='Logout'> next to Club Manager chip. Player sign-out = click user chip 'cooler74.ea@gmail.com' → 'Logout' menu item. 'Notifications' sidebar label includes a badge count so use `page.get_by_text('Notifications', exact=False)` or scope by data-testid instead.
rca_of_the_issue: ORIGINAL BUG (from previous iteration): while on any non-default tab (Schedule, Payments, …), the dashboard silently reverted to Dashboard/Overview after ~55 min because (a) Supabase `onAuthStateChange` fires on every token refresh with a NEW User object of the SAME identity, which re-referenced user.user_metadata; (b) PlayerDashboard's `refreshClubPlayer` useCallback depended on user.user_metadata, so its identity churned every refresh; (c) the initial-load useEffect depended on that callback, so it re-ran; (d) any transient null-session flicker briefly triggered navigate('/player/login') → the dashboard remounted → activeTab reset to 'overview'. FIX APPLIED IN THIS ITERATION'S CODE (verified in reading /src/lib/AuthContext.tsx, /src/pages/PlayerDashboard.tsx, /src/pages/ManagerDashboard.tsx): (A) AuthContext setUser now dedupes on id+email and only emits dlbc-auth-change on identity-changing events; (B) PlayerDashboard's activeTab is persisted to sessionStorage and its initial-load effect deps reduced to [navigate]; (C) ManagerDashboard's activeView persisted to sessionStorage; (D) forceSignOut clears both sessionStorage keys. Verified end-to-end: after `supabase.auth.refreshSession()` the active tab / view / sidebar highlight / heading all remain on Schedule, and after a hard reload the same view reopens; sign-out cleans up both keys.
